descript.abstract: This dataset provides USFS watershed improvement activities to barriers to upstream migration. This includes improving existing passage structures or removing them entirely. Structures include culverts, dams, diversion dams. Also included are where structures have been added to purposely create barriers to protect native populations from invasive species. Data include the planned fiscal year and planned cost, the completed fiscal year, approximate completed cost, and partners involved. Each AOP activity is displayed as a single point.
descript.purpose: Provide spatial and tabular information about USFS activities aimed at improving or protecting Aquatic Organism Passage (AOP) from the Natural Resource Manager Watershed Improvement Tracking (WIT) database.

timeperd.current: Data are current as of publication date

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse
cntorgp.cntper: National Applications Liaison Office | US Forest Service (usda.gov)

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: This data is based on field gathered observations. No specific quality check or measures are available.

horizpa.horizpar: Horizontal accuracy is variable No specific quality check or measures are available.

procstep.procdesc: The source of this dataset is the Natural Resource Manager (NRM) database of the US Forest Service. The aquatic organism passage data are pulled from the oracle view, fs_nris_wit.nrwi_aop_activities_vw. This data is published using Feature Manipulation Engine Software. The spatial features were extracted from the transactional feature class fs_nris_wit.nris_wit_site_pt and published to the target feature class at EDW.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: USFS Region

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value of the USFS region. See https://www.fs.usda.gov/about-agency/contact-us/regional-offices for more info

attr.attrdef: Forest Unit ID responsible for the activity.

attrdomv.udom: 4 digit numerical code. List of codes and their spatial extents can be viewed on the USFS AGOL site here: https://usfs.maps.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=09e4c1162a4d4af3a84163cbc76108c4

attr.attrdef: Forest Unit Name responsible for the activity.

attrdomv.udom: Text values. List of USFS Forest Unit names and their spatial extents can be viewed on the USFS AGOL site here: https://usfs.maps.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=09e4c1162a4d4af3a84163cbc76108c4

attr.attrdef: User provided unique ID for the activity.
User provided descriptive name for the activity.
An abbreviated name for the type of activity taking place at the site.
User provided name for the location of the activity.
Fiscal year the activity was planned to take place at the site.
Estimate of the total activity costs that are planned for a particular year. This includes design and implementation costs but excludes NEPA and analysis costs.
Miles of stream expected to be made accessible to aquatic species by improving the stream crossing.
The fiscal year the activity was completed on the ground. Fiscal year begins October 1 and ends September 30.
Date the activity was completed on the ground.
Miles of stream habitat made accessible to aquatic species by improving the stream crossing.
Total approximate cost to implement the activity. This is the sum of FS Cost, Partner Cost, and Partner In-Kind Contribution.
The approximate monetary contribution from US Forest Service of the total estimated cost.
The approximate monetary contribution from partners of the total estimated cost.
The estimated non-monetary contribution from partners of the total estimated cost.
Indicates whether the activity received contributions from partners. (Y/N)
List of partner names that contributed to the activity.
Indicates how the feature was created, associated to a travel route, associated to an NHDFlowline or other (digitized or copied from another layer).

edom.edomvd: Geometry based on INFRA Roads Travel Route

attr.attrdef: The road number
Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the Travel Route from Infra Roads, if present.
Distance along the travel route in miles.
Unique ID for the NHD Flowline.
Distance along the NHD route as a percent.
Type of plan phase

edom.edomvd: The Activity is in Implementation Phase.

attr.attrdef: Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the spatial feature.
Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the activity.
Unique system generated Control Number (CN) for the plan phase.
Feature geometry.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse
cntorgp.cntper: National Applications Liaison Office | US Forest Service (usda.gov)

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
